articles.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
articles.summary: The third-party website exposed applicants' sensitive documents as part of the U.K. visa application process. Instead of fixing the issue, the company sent attorneys.

articles.title: What we’re looking for in Startup Battlefield 2026, and how to apply in time for the May 27 deadline
articles.summary: Startup Battlefield applications are due tomorrow, so now's the time to put the finishing touches on your submission!

articles.title: Trump administration permits Volvo to keep selling connected cars in the US
articles.summary: Volvo, which is majority owned by China's Geely Holdings, said it can now move forward with its expansion plans for its U.S. factory.

articles.title: Trump administration wants nuclear startups to use plutonium for their reactors
articles.summary: The U.S. government is sitting on dozens of tons of weapons-grade plutonium. It's hoping startups can find a use for it.

articles.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
articles.summary: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

articles.title: Starship’s path to reusability looks murky after SpaceX’s S-1
articles.summary: SpaceX's recent IPO and Starship rocket test flight delivered two big data points that offer a realistic vision for the coming years — and one that may disappoint both the company's boosters and its critics.

articles.title: SpaceX’s Starlink nabs American Airlines contract, another win for its IPO
articles.summary: American Airlines said Tuesday it plans to install Starlink on more than 500 Airbus aircraft, the latest carrier win for IPO-bound SpaceX.

articles.title: OpenRouter more than doubles valuation to $1.3B in a year
articles.summary: OpenRouter has raised a $113 million Series B led by CapitalG. Its 5x growth in usage over six months indicates the multi-AI-model future is here.

articles.title: The IPO Comeback Has A Catch
articles.summary: The threshold for going public has steadily risen, leaving many mid-sized firms and their shareholders without viable exit opportunities, writes guest author Shawn Bercuson, founder of Earlyasset, who contends it has created growing pressure for a more mature private secondary market.

articles.title: In Charts: Seed Deals Keep Getting Bigger As Odds Of Reaching Series A Fall Dramatically
articles.summary: Seed rounds are larger than ever, with some startups now raising $8 million to $10 million deals once associated with later stages. But the path forward has also become tougher.

articles.title: Trump spent 3 hours at Walter Reed. he claims ‘everything checked out PERFECTLY’
articles.summary: Four exams in 16 months, a condition that pools blood in his legs, and a poll showing most Americans doubt his fitness: the presidential fitness test.

articles.title: Sam Altman and Dario Amodei are both walking back their AI jobs apocalypse prophecies as they eye blockbuster IPOs
articles.summary: Goldman's David Solomon and Box’s Aaron Levie have been saying that there won’t be a white-collar wipeout. Altman says he's "delighted to be wrong."

articles.title: After receiving backlash, the CEO of Standard Chartered apologizes for hurt over saying ‘lower value human capital’ will be automated by AI
articles.summary: Bill Winters, the CEO of Standard Chartered, says about 15% of “back office” corporate function roles will be reduced in the next four years.

articles.title: Uber drivers in Massachusetts just pulled off the biggest labor win since 1941 — just before the robots arrive
articles.summary: Jean Fredo drives 60-hour weeks terrified of losing app access with no appeal. Now he has a union. So does everyone watching from California.

The attacker who hit the most financial services organizations over the past 12 months never phished a password. They called an IT support line, convinced an employee to reset their MFA, and registered their own device on the network.

articles.summary: Amid rapidly growing adoption of enterprise-level AI agents, there’s a disconnect emerging between ambition and execution.  Although 85% of organizations say they want to be agentic within the next three years, 76% say their current operations and infrastructure can’t support that change. articles.title: 经过华为、传音、拓竹历练，95后打造AI母婴界特斯拉｜36氪首发
articles.summary:
作者 | 张子怡

编辑 | 袁斯来

硬氪获悉，AI母婴智能硬件品牌珂迪Coddie（以下简称“Coddie”）完成天使轮融资，融资金额数千万元，由钟鼎资本投资，向阳资本担任独家财务顾问。

Coddie团队于2026年初组建，由95后连续创业者何鋡威创立。何鋡威毕业于德国慕尼黑工业大学，先后任职华为、传音控股、拓竹科技等全球化科技企业。技术负责人毕业于国内顶级高校计算机专业，曾就职半导体龙头上市公司，负责大疆与Insta360影像IC方案落地；后加入国家“小巨人”第三代半导体企业担任产品负责人，具

articles.title: 化险为夷的洛克王国，给所有长青游戏上了一课
articles.summary:
文丨果脯

2026年5月21日，腾讯魔方工作室群开发的《洛克王国：世界》上线了S2赛季更新。这款基于13年经典IP、以精灵收集养成为核心的大世界游戏，在2026年3月底正式上线后迅速成为年度爆款。据官方数据，游戏上线9天用户量便突破3000万，全平台预约人数达6000万，DAU达到1300万。

articles.title: 36氪首发｜男士美妆品牌「GREENLAB绿所」获宝顶创投战略投资，张耀东出任公司董事
articles.summary:
36氪获悉，无感化面部理容品牌「GREENLAB绿所」（以下简称“绿所”）近日宣布完成千万级新一轮融资，本轮由宝顶创投战略投资。融资完成后，宝顶创投创始合伙人张耀东将出任绿所公司董事，参与品牌战略、产品矩阵、渠道经营等方面的长期建设。

这也是继2025年完成Pre-A轮融资后，绿所在半年左右时间内再次获得外部资本加注。据了解，本轮资金将主要用于新品研发量产、品牌建设、渠道经营及组织能力提升等方面。

绿所是36氪持续关注的品牌，定位“无感化面部理容”，重点面向“精致懒”的年轻男性用户。所谓“无感化”，指的是用户在购买、使用和呈现效果上都尽

articles.title: 硅谷AI一线观察：一人花掉50万美金Token背后的大厂焦虑
articles.summary:
文｜周鑫雨 邓咏仪

硅谷持续了两个多月的Token-Maxxing（Token军备竞赛）焦虑，在Meta以一种戏谑的方式按下了休止符。

articles.summary:
文｜周鑫雨

编辑｜张雨忻

打开“观猹”，能看到一个完全不同于机构投资榜单所呈现出来的AI创业生态：

这里很少有动辄估值几亿美金的项目，活跃在社区中的，也常常不是有着名校、大厂背景的创业者，更多是普通的设计师、产品经理，甚至退休教师。
